title: 10 best herbal teas for dehydration
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dehydration are infusions made from various dried plants, flowers, and seeds, which can help replenish fluids in the body and alleviate symptoms of dehydration. These teas are beneficial for hydration due to their high water content and mild flavor, which can encourage individuals to drink more fluids. Moreover, some herbal teas contain natural electrolytes and sugars that can help the body absorb and retain water more effectively than plain water alone. For instance, herbal teas can be a refreshing and healthier alternative to sugary sports drinks for athletes seeking to rehydrate after intense workouts. Additionally, for individuals who struggle to drink enough water throughout the day, sipping on herbal teas can help them meet their daily fluid intake needs while also enjoying a flavorful and soothing beverage. Overall, herbal teas for dehydration can provide a natural and effective way to stay hydrated and maintain optimal health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chia tea helps with dehydration because it contains chia seeds, which can absorb up to 10 times their weight in water. This unique property allows chia seeds to help retain moisture in the body, thus aiding in hydration. Moreover, chia tea is often flavored with herbs like mint, lemon balm, or rosehip, which can provide additional electrolytes and antioxidants. Drinking chia tea can contribute to maintaining a healthy fluid balance, making it a natural and tasty option for staying hydrated.
remedy_properties: High water content: Chia tea is hydrating due to its high water content, helping to replenish fluids lost through dehydration.
Electrolyte balance: Chia seeds contain essential electrolytes such as potassium, calcium, and magnesium, which help maintain proper fluid balance and are crucial for rehydration.
Antioxidant properties: Chia tea's antioxidants protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can be exacerbated during dehydration.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Chia seeds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate inflammation caused by dehydration, particularly in the digestive tract.
Fiber-rich: Chia seeds are a great source of dietary fiber, which aids in digestion and helps the body absorb water and nutrients more effectively, improving overall hydration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in electrolytes and antioxidants, chia tea leaves help rehydrate the body and protect cells from damage caused by dehydration.
Seeds: Chia seeds absorb up to 10 times their weight in water, slowly releasing it back into the body, making them an excellent natural remedy for dehydration.
Flowers: Chia tea flowers contain essential oils and flavonoids that help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract, improving water absorption and alleviating dehydration symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 tablespoon of chia seeds and soak them in 2 cups of water for at least 30 minutes to create a gel-like consistency.
Finely chop 1 tablespoon of dried hydrangea root and add it to the soaked chia seeds.
Thinly slice 1 tablespoon of dried elderberries and stir them into the mixture.
Bring the concoction to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea, allowing it to cool before drinking, and consume 2-3 times a day for dehydration relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavender tea helps with dehydration because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help the body recover from the effects of dehydration. The herbal tea is known for its soothing and calming effects, which can help regulate the body's temperature and reduce fever, both of which can contribute to dehydration. Moreover, lavender tea can help stimulate the production of urine, which can aid in flushing out toxins and excess fluids from the body. Additionally, lavender tea has a pleasant taste, making it an appealing alternative to plain water for those who struggle to stay hydrated. Drinking lavender tea can also help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which can further support the body's natural hydration processes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the gut, promoting better nutrient absorption and hydration. This is mainly due to the presence of linalyl acetate and linalool.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of lavender tea help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, aiding in overall cellular health during dehydration. This is attributed to the high levels of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic acids.

Anxiolytic: Lavender tea's ability to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation can help manage stress-induced dehydration by decreasing stress hormone levels. The primary constituents responsible for this effect are linalool and lavandulol.
Diuretic: Lavender tea can help increase urine production, which aids in removing toxins and excess fluids from the body. This is due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids.
Digestive aid: Lavender tea can help alleviate digestive issues, promoting better nutrient absorption and hydration. This is mainly due to the essential oils and tannins found in lavender.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils and tannins, lavender leaves help to replace lost fluids and soothe the body during dehydration.
Flowers: High in antioxidants and volatile compounds, lavender flowers aid in rehydration and support the body's recovery from dehydration.
Buds: Packed with soothing properties, lavender buds provide a gentle and effective way to combat dehydration symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried culinary lavender flowers, depending on preferred strength.
Boil 8 ounces of fresh water and let cool for 1-2 minutes to ensure optimal temperature.
Place lavender flowers in a tea infuser or teapot and pour hot water over them.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Remove the infuser or strain the tea, then enjoy it while warm to alleviate dehydration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with dehydration because it contains menthol, a natural compound that can help soothe and relax the gastrointestinal tract, promoting better fluid absorption. Additionally, peppermint tea is caffeine-free and made from the infusion of peppermint leaves in hot water, making it a healthy and hydrating alternative to sugary drinks or caffeinated beverages. The tea also has a refreshing and cooling effect, which can help quench thirst and reduce the desire to drink less healthy beverages. Furthermore, peppermint tea has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help support overall health and well-being, making it an excellent choice for staying hydrated and promoting overall health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, can help reduce inflammation in the gut, promoting water and electrolyte absorption.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic nature of peppermint tea, owing to menthol and rosmarinic acid, alleviates gastrointestinal cramps and spasms, enabling better nutrient and fluid uptake.
Carminative: Peppermint tea's carminative effects, resulting from menthol and methyl salicylate, help relieve gas and bloating, making it easier for the body to rehydrate.
Diaphoretic: Peppermint tea's diaphoretic properties, attributed to menthol, encourage perspiration, which in turn helps cool the body and replace lost fluids during dehydration.

Analgesic: The analgesic properties of peppermint tea, stemming from menthol and flavonoids, can help alleviate headaches and other aches associated with dehydration, offering comfort and promoting rehydration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in volatile oils, including menthol, which can help stimulate sweating and cool the body, aiding in rehydration.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids that can help improve fluid balance in the body and have mild diuretic properties, supporting hydration.
Stems: While less potent than leaves and flowers, stems can still contribute to the overall hydrating effect of the tea, providing a source of antioxidants and trace minerals.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the peppermint leaves for 5-10 minutes to allow the beneficial compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea to remove the spent peppermint leaves.
Allow the tea to cool slightly to a safe drinking temperature.
Sip the herbal peppermint tea slowly to rehydrate and soothe your body.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosehip tea helps with dehydration because it is rich in vitamin C and antioxidants, which can help the body absorb and retain water more effectively. Additionally, rosehip tea contains electrolytes, such as potassium and magnesium, that are essential for maintaining proper fluid balance in the body. The tea is also naturally caffeine-free, making it a healthier alternative to sugary sports drinks or caffeinated beverages that can actually contribute to dehydration. Drinking rosehip tea can help replenish fluids and provide essential nutrients to support overall health and hydration.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosehip tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to high levels of vitamin C and polyphenols, can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can be exacerbated by dehydration.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including vitamin C, flavonoids, and carotenoids, helps protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can increase during dehydration.
Diuretic: Rosehip tea has a mild diuretic effect, promoting urination and helping the body eliminate toxins and excess fluids, thus aiding in rehydration.
Immune support: The high vitamin C content in rosehip tea supports the immune system, which can be weakened by dehydration.
Digestive aid: Rosehip tea can help soothe digestive issues, such as constipation and bloating, that can be exacerbated by dehydration, due to its mild laxative effect and high fiber content.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in vitamins and minerals, rosehip seeds can help rehydrate the body and replenish essential nutrients lost during dehydration.
Leaves: Containing tannins that can help the body absorb and retain water, rosehip leaves are often used in medicinal teas for dehydration.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, rosehip flowers can help protect cells from damage caused by dehydration and promote overall health.
Rhyzomes: Rosehip rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation caused by dehydration and promote healthy fluid balance.
Bark: High in pectin, rosehip bark can help thicken medicinal teas and slow down the absorption of water, providing long-lasting hydration.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried rosehips and 1 cup of water.
Bring the water to a boil in a pot or kettle.
Place the dried rosehips in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the rosehips and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea, then drink immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hibiscus tea helps with dehydration because it contains high levels of antioxidants and electrolytes, which are essential for maintaining the body's fluid balance. The tea is made from the vibrant flowers of the hibiscus plant, and it has a refreshing, slightly tart flavor that can help quench thirst. Moreover, hibiscus tea is caffeine-free and can be enjoyed hot or cold, making it a versatile and hydrating beverage option. Its natural diuretic properties can also help the body eliminate toxins and excess fluids, further supporting healthy hydration levels.
remedy_properties: Diuretic: Hibiscus tea helps to combat dehydration by promoting diuresis, or the production of urine, which helps to eliminate toxins and excess fluids from the body. This is due to the presence of anthocyanins and other polyphenols.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of hibiscus tea help to protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can be exacerbated by dehydration. The active constituents responsible for these antioxidant effects include anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

Electrolyte balance: Hibiscus tea contains a variety of minerals, including potassium, calcium, and magnesium, which can help to maintain electrolyte balance and prevent dehydration-induced electrolyte imbalances.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of hibiscus tea can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which can be exacerbated by dehydration. The active constituents responsible for these anti-inflammatory effects include anthocyanins, flavonoids, and other polyphenols.

Vitamin C content: Hibiscus tea is rich in vitamin C, which can help to boost the immune system and support overall health during periods of dehydration. Vitamin C also acts as an antioxidant, helping to protect cells from damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in electrolytes and antioxidants, hibiscus leaves help rehydrate the body and fight free radicals.
Flowers: High in vitamin C and minerals, hibiscus flowers promote fluid balance and support the immune system during dehydration.
Calyces: The outer layer of the hibiscus flower, called the calyx, is a powerhouse of hydration, containing polyphenols and flavonoids that help retain water in the body.
Sepals: The protective layer of the hibiscus flower bud (sepal) is a great source of mucilage, which soothes the digestive system and aids in water absorption.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried hibiscus flowers and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 4 cups of water and pour it over the hibiscus flowers, allowing them to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the hibiscus flowers.
Optional: Add honey or lemon to taste for added flavor and added electrolytes.
Serve the tea hot or refrigerate it to serve cold, drinking the entire 4 cups throughout the day to help with dehydration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with dehydration because it contains high levels of antioxidants and electrolytes, which are essential for rehydration. The ginger root used in the tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe the stomach, making it an ideal beverage for those who are dehydrated due to vomiting or diarrhea. Additionally, ginger tea is caffeine-free, making it a healthier alternative to sugary sports drinks or caffeinated beverages that can further dehydrate the body. Drinking warm ginger tea can also help stimulate perspiration, which can help cool down the body and replace lost fluids. Overall, ginger tea is a natural and effective way to rehydrate and restore balance to the body.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to compounds like gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce inflammation in the gut, promoting better nutrient and fluid absorption.
Antiemetic: Ginger tea's antiemetic properties, resulting from gingerols and shogaols, can help alleviate nausea and vomiting, common symptoms that exacerbate dehydration.
Diaphoretic: Ginger tea's diaphoretic properties, attributed to gingerols, increase perspiration, which in turn helps cool the body and restore electrolyte balance.
Immune-boosting: Ginger tea's immune-boosting properties, owing to its high antioxidant content, can help protect the body from infections, reducing the risk of dehydration caused by illness.
Digestive aid: Ginger tea's digestive aid properties, due to gingerols and shogaols, can improve digestion and nutrient absorption, maximizing fluid retention and reducing dehydration.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are commonly used in medicinal tea due to their high concentration of electrolytes and ability to help the body absorb fluids, making them ideal for treating dehydration.
Leaves: Ginger leaves are often used in tea for their hydrating properties, as they contain high levels of antioxidants and essential nutrients that can help replenish lost fluids.
Flowers: Ginger flowers are sometimes used in medicinal tea for dehydration due to their ability to soothe the digestive system and promote fluid absorption.
Seeds: Ginger seeds are used in tea for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and promote fluid balance.
remedy_recipe: Peel and chop 1 inch of fresh ginger root into small pieces, then place in a pot.
Add 4 cups of water to the pot and bring it to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, then add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day for dehydration relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus tea helps with dehydration because it contains high levels of antioxidants and tannins that can help the body absorb fluids more effectively. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritation in the throat and respiratory system, which can be helpful for those who are dehydrated due to illness. Additionally, eucalyptus tea has a cooling effect on the body, which can help regulate body temperature and reduce sweating, thus helping to prevent further dehydration. Drinking eucalyptus tea can also help stimulate the production of saliva and digestive juices, which can help the body absorb water and nutrients more efficiently. Overall, eucalyptus tea can be a delicious and effective way to stay hydrated and support overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eucalyptus tea helps reduce inflammation, which is important for dehydration as it can reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat and mouth, making it easier to drink fluids. The main active constituents responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties are cineole and other eucalyptus oils.

Expectorant: Eucalyptus tea aids in expelling mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, which is crucial for dehydration as it helps clear obstructions and promotes better breathing and fluid intake. The expectorant properties are primarily due to the presence of cineole.

Antimicrobial: Eucalyptus tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria, which is significant for dehydration as it can help prevent infections and reduce inflammation in the digestive and respiratory tracts, allowing for better nutrient absorption and fluid balance. The antimicrobial effects are attributed to eucalyptus oils, particularly cineole and aromadendrene.

Diaphoretic: Eucalyptus tea promotes sweating, which is beneficial for dehydration as it helps regulate body temperature, eliminate toxins, and rehydrate the body through the skin. The diaphoretic properties are mainly due to the presence of cineole and other eucalyptus oils.

Analgesic: Eucalyptus tea helps alleviate pain, which is important for dehydration as it can reduce discomfort and encourage fluid intake. The analgesic properties are primarily attributed to cineole and other eucalyptus oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the eucalyptus tea plant are rich in tannins and flavonoids, which can help to alleviate dehydration by reducing inflammation and promoting the absorption of water into the body.
Stems: The stems of the eucalyptus tea plant contain high levels of essential oils, which can help to stimulate the production of sweat and promote fluid balance in the body.
Bark: The bark of the eucalyptus tea plant is a good source of antioxidants and can help to protect the body against damage from free radicals, which can contribute to dehydration.
Buds: The buds of the eucalyptus tea plant are rich in tannins and can help to soothe a dry, irritated throat and promote hydration.

Flowers: The flowers of the eucalyptus tea plant contain essential oils that can help to open up the airways and promote easy breathing, which can be beneficial for those experiencing dehydration due to respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 cup of boiling water with 1-2 teaspoons of dried eucalyptus leaves.
Steep the mixture, covered, for 5-10 minutes to allow the nutrients to infuse.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, pouring the liquid into a cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, aiming to consume at least 2-3 cups throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with dehydration because it contains high levels of antioxidants and electrolytes, which are essential for maintaining the body's fluid balance. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties can also help soothe irritated throat and mouth tissues caused by dehydration. Moreover, sage tea has a diuretic effect that promotes urination, aiding the body in eliminating toxins and excess fluids. This natural beverage is a healthy and refreshing way to stay hydrated while enjoying its subtle, earthy flavor.
remedy_properties: Anti-diuretic: Sage tea can help counteract dehydration by reducing the frequency of urination, allowing the body to retain more water. This is due to the presence of saffron yellow pigments and essential oils such as thujone and camphor.
Electrolyte balance: Sage tea contains essential minerals like potassium, calcium, and magnesium, which help replenish electrolytes lost through dehydration.

Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to its rosmarinic acid and flavonoid content, can help alleviate inflammation in the gut, promoting better nutrient absorption and water retention.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in sage tea, primarily from its rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, supports overall cellular health and boosts the immune system during dehydration.

Digestive aid: Sage tea stimulates the digestive system, improving nutrient absorption and reducing the risk of gastrointestinal issues that can exacerbate dehydration. This is facilitated by the presence of volatile oils like cineole, borneol, and camphor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential electrolytes, the leaves help replenish the body's fluids during dehydration.
Stems: Containing trace amounts of valuable minerals, the stems help restore balance in the body during dehydration.
Flowers: With natural anti-inflammatory properties, the flowers can help soothe irritation caused by dehydration.
Seeds: High in healthy fats and antioxidants, the seeds can help protect cells from damage caused by dehydration.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot for 3-5 minutes.
Crush 1-2 teaspoons of dried sage leaves and add them to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the crushed sage leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup to discard the sage leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink the sage tea while it's still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with dehydration because it contains high levels of antioxidants and electrolytes, which are essential for rehydration. The herbal tea is a natural source of fluid that can help restore the body's water balance, while the antioxidants present in thyme can aid in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress caused by dehydration. Additionally, thyme tea has mild diuretic properties that can help eliminate toxins from the body, further supporting the rehydration process. Drinking thyme tea can also soothe a dry throat and relieve symptoms of dehydration such as headaches and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tea has anti-inflammatory compounds, including rosmarinic acid and carvacrol, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the body caused by dehydration.

Diaphoretic properties: Thyme tea stimulates perspiration, promoting the removal of toxins and rehydrating the body during dehydration. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as thymol and carvacrol.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of harmful bacteria and maintain a healthy gut flora during dehydration. The main active constituents responsible for this property are thymol and carvacrol.
Vitamin and mineral content: Thyme tea contains vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C and potassium, which support the immune system and help maintain electrolyte balance during dehydration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antioxidants and essential oils, which can help replenish fluids and electrolytes in the body.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids that have diuretic properties, aiding in the treatment of dehydration by promoting fluid balance.
Stems: Thyme stems contain tannins that can help soothe irritated tissues in the mouth and throat, providing relief during dehydration.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a teapot or saucepan.
Steep the thyme leaves in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.

The herb is also known for its diuretic properties, which help to flush out toxins and promote urination, thereby aiding in the elimination of excess fluids from the body. Furthermore, basil tea has a soothing effect on the stomach and intestines, making it an effective remedy for treating dehydration caused by stomach bugs or food poisoning. Drinking basil tea can help to replenish the body's fluids, providing quick relief from dehydration symptoms such as dizziness, fatigue, and headaches.
remedy_properties: Rehydration: Basil tea can help replenish lost fluids due to dehydration, as it contains essential electrolytes and antioxidants that support the body's hydration process (active constituents

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of basil tea can reduce inflammation in the body, which is often exacerbated by dehydration, by suppressing the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines (active constituents
Antispasmodic: Basil tea's antispasmodic properties can help alleviate muscle cramps and spasms caused by dehydration by relaxing smooth muscles and reducing muscle contractions (active constituents

remedy_parts: Leaves: Basil leaves are rich in electrolytes and antioxidants, making them ideal for rehydrating the body and fighting free radicals.
Stems: Basil stems contain essential oils that can help regulate the body's water balance and promote healthy digestion.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried basil leaves and rinse 1 cup of fresh basil leaves to remove any impurities.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dehydration with herbal teas is by consuming infusions made from hydrating herbs, such as chamomile, lemon balm, or honeybush. These teas are not only caffeine-free, but they also have a soothing effect on the body. Additionally, consider adding a pinch of salt and a squeeze of lemon to your tea to help replenish electrolytes lost through dehydration. Drink these teas consistently throughout the day to maintain optimal hydration levels and alleviate dehydration symptoms.
supplementary_causes: The primary causes of dehydration that are treatable with herbal teas are strenuous physical activities, illnesses such as fever, vomiting, and diarrhea, and environmental factors like hot weather and high altitudes. These conditions can lead to fluid loss, which, if not replenished, can result in dehydration. Herbal teas, with their hydrating properties, can help restore the body's fluid balance and provide essential nutrients and antioxidants, aiding in the prevention and treatment of dehydration.
supplementary_preparations: Other herbal preparations to use with teas for dehydration are tinctures, capsules, and glycerites. Tinctures are alcohol-based extractions of herbs, taken in small amounts for their potent effects. Capsules provide a measured dose of dried herbs in a convenient form. Glycerites are liquid extracts made with vegetable glycerin, offering a sweet-tasting, alcohol-free alternative for children and those avoiding alcohol. Incorporating these preparations can complement hydration efforts, addressing the underlying causes of dehydration.
supplementary_frequency: For dehydration, you should take herbal teas every two hours. This frequency ensures that your body receives a consistent supply of hydration and electrolytes. Each tea serving, typically 8 ounces, can replace a portion of the fluids lost due to dehydration. This regimen helps maintain your body's fluid balance, supporting essential functions and preventing worsening dehydration. However, it's important to note that herbal teas should complement, not replace, proper fluid intake through water and other beverages.
supplementary_side_effects: The possible side effects associated with consuming herbal teas for dehydration are stomach irritation, nausea, and allergic reactions. Some herbal teas can interact with medications, worsening pre-existing medical conditions. Pregnant women should avoid certain herbal teas due to potential risks. Overconsumption can lead to electrolyte imbalance, as some herbal teas have diuretic properties. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before incorporating herbal teas into a dehydration treatment plan.
